summary: Comprehensive child-safe visual generation pipeline with defense-in-depth style enforcement, dual moderation gates (prompt text + generated image), re-moderation on apply, and proper tool registry integration. 2 medium + 3 low findings.

findings.title: _preview_cache is unbounded and never pruned for abandoned previews
findings.detail: Each request_preview adds an entry to _preview_cache. Only apply_preview and discard_preview remove entries. A user who previews many images without applying or discarding will accumulate stale entries indefinitely. This follows the same pattern flagged in TASK-028's _pending_mutations.
findings.recommendation: Add a MAX_PREVIEW_CACHE constant (e.g., 20) and evict oldest entries when exceeded, or add time-based expiry using the clock dependency.

findings.title: _build_preview_id uses non-cryptographic hash with collision risk
findings.detail: _build_preview_id generates 'preview_%d' via absi(seed.hash()). GDScript .hash() is not collision-resistant. Two different project/world/asset/style combinations could produce the same preview_id, causing the second preview to overwrite the first in _preview_cache. On apply, the wrong image bytes could be persisted.
findings.recommendation: Incorporate a monotonic counter or timestamp in the preview_id to guarantee uniqueness within a session.

findings.title: Photoreal-human term lists diverge between service and orchestration
findings.detail: VisualAssetGenerationService.PHOTOREAL_HUMAN_TERMS includes 'fotorealistyczny', 'realistyczny czlowiek', 'portret czlowieka'. RequestAICreationHelpService._contains_human_visual_term includes 'person', 'osoba' which are absent from the service list. Conversely, the service has Polish compound terms the orchestration lacks. This inconsistency means some prompts may pass one gate but not the other depending on the code path.
findings.recommendation: Extract a shared PHOTOREAL_HUMAN_TERMS constant into the shared kernel or a policy class referenced by both. Ensure both English and Polish terms are in both lists.

findings.title: _resolve_style_for_actor uses duck-typing for generator capabilities
findings.detail: _resolve_style_for_actor checks _visual_generator.has_method('get_allowed_styles') to dynamically query the adapter's style list. This is not part of the VisualGenerationPort contract, so different adapters may silently skip this path.
findings.recommendation: Add get_allowed_styles() -> Array[String] to VisualGenerationPort with a default returning the CHILD_SAFE_STYLES constant, or pass allowed styles explicitly via setup().

findings.title: Language hardcoded to 'pl-PL' in generate_image call
findings.detail: The generate_image call at line 96 passes 'pl-PL' as a literal string. While Polish-first is the project default, this ignores the localization policy port used elsewhere. If the engine ever supports other locales, this would need updating.
findings.recommendation: Consider injecting LocalizationPolicyPort and using get_locale() for consistency, or document that visual generation is Polish-only by design.

positive_observations: Defense-in-depth: style enforcement at both orchestration (tool validation) and service level
Dual moderation gates: check_text on prompt AND check_image on generated bytes before preview
Re-moderation on apply — prevents TOCTOU where moderation policy changes between preview and apply
Comprehensive photoreal-human blocking with English + Polish term lists for kid mode
PNG magic bytes in test adapter ensures format-aware moderation compatibility
Clean preview/apply/discard lifecycle with deterministic preview IDs
Watermark metadata tag 'ai_visual' on all generated assets for downstream identification
SafetyInterventionTriggeredEvent emission for all block paths with specific policy rules
visual_generate schema in AIToolRegistry with strict required args and no unknown args allowed
11 contract assertions covering preview, apply, photoreal blocking, re-moderation on apply, and event emission
